title: potentilla anserina subvar. aurantiaca
intro: Potentilla anserina subvar. aurantiaca, also known as Orange Marsh Cinquefoil, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North America, with medicinal properties attributed to its roots, stems, and leaves. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Potentilla anserina subvar. aurantiaca can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and promote overall well-being.
